author: gleaning
text:
Vendor minimal SHA-1; drop sha1 + hex crates

Follow-on to the bl-bd85 decision (epic bl-32f8): vendor a small in-tree SHA-1 so the RustCrypto chain leaves the dependency tree while preserving the byte-identical hash values that task ids and stealth-store directories depend on as on-disk state.

src/hash.rs (~80 lines, FIPS 180-4) provides sha1_hex(&[u8]) -> lowercase hex, covered by four canonical FIPS vectors: empty, abc, 56-byte two-block-padding, and 1M-'a' multi-block. Output is byte-identical to hex::encode(sha1::Sha1::digest), so existing ids and stealth stores keep resolving the same way.

Both call sites (Task::generate_id, store_paths::stealth_tasks_dir) switched over with their kept-exception comments updated to point at the vendored module. Normal dep tree drops from 39 -> 30 crates (sha1, hex, digest, block-buffer, crypto-common, generic-array, typenum, cpufeatures, cfg-if). 100% coverage maintained.
